reviews.review_description: This has great potential.... the flying bit feels very good. It would be good to see it better optimised for the Go and a few added features like auto pilot and gamepad control. Keep going with it though

developer_response.body: Thank you for your review! Yes, there will be a Go update soon. Regarding autopilot is currently available while playing without the remote. You are welcome to contact me through my webpage and share more of your thoughts.

reviews.review_title: There are no graphic of building
reviews.review_description: Oh my god,this is a bad game have been i have everseen,because there are only square graphic,there are no building.my phone is s7 edge

developer_response.body: Hi, I understand you have an issue with the buildings. please contact me through my page and I'll try to help you solve the issue. Make sure your internet is running fine because you won't see any buildings without a proper internet connection.

reviews.review_description: Nice app!

I love the way you can fly in this game. It's simple to pick up with cool tricks to master. I love how the game uses real maps to realistically fly over landmarks! It is also impressive that all of this was built by one person! That's amazing!!!

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot! I am really happy to hear.

reviews.review_description: This app ran smooth on my Galaxy 8 and worked well with the controller.  It is a lot of fun to fly and explore the different cities.  Good job!

This could be the start of something big. It is a great concept but still needs some development. I would also state it appears to be made for vr gear rather than Oculus go. Not perfect and it cries out for gamepad compatibility, this alone would be a big improvement. I really want to like it more than I do. I cant quite decide if it is supposed to be an arcade game or a simulator and maybe that is part of the problem. I had my moneys worth out of it the night I bought it, but not sure if I will use it a lot more.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for your review!
SkyTrek was built initially for Gear VR, yet I am continuously working to make SkyTrek better. In the next updates, I will have improvements for Oculus Go users, as well.

reviews.review_title: Needs some work, has great potential.
reviews.review_description: This game just feels like it’s a little unpolished, experienced some weird glitches like starting off in the ground. I really enjoy the controls feels like you have some good control on the aircraft not that it’s very in depth. The fact it uses real maps to draw out the levels is pretty amazing, wondering why the Grand Canyon is full of trees too, cool concept hope to see it thrive.
Right now it needs alot of work. Its glitchy, automatic

developer_response.body: Thanks! I am always working on updates, you are welcome to contact me with details about your experience.

reviews.review_description: I thoroughly enjoyed playing this.  A few glitches... Chicago mission the landing strip is below the ground level and Toronto takeoff ground is a little glitshy... things i would like to see on a update is accurate air speed displayed and interactive... what i mean is if you dive then speed goes up if you climb then it goes down and you don't imidiatly slow down when you slow engine down.... the field of view needs to be bigger to increase the experience... in free flight it would be nice to have a location search instead of only having gps coordinates...

developer_response.body:
Thanks a lot for your review! Your comments will be taken into full consideration on the upcoming update.
- Working on a better flight experience with engine behavior with climb and dive is a top priority.
- You can increase the field of view in the Settings tab, but this may cause a drop of frame rate. This is a very important issue, and I am pushing it for max results with the limitations of the mobile hardware to the date.
- Chicago and Toronto issue is under investigation and will be fixed shortly.

reviews.review_description: I hope that the developer adds quest support soon.

I really enjoyed flying over London. All the sights were there.  Flying is really easy with the gear vr controller. The flight simulator isn't too serious and is very forgiving so enjoy doing some nauseating acrobatics!  The music is great and made me smile. 

The game seems to push my S7 to the limit with rendering the scenery . But I didn't let that detract from the fun.

Very glad I have this game and will spend quite a bit of time with it and look forward to further updates!
I absolutely love this app, but it can't see my location (Oculus GO) please fix this

developer_response.body: Thanks for your review, I am checking this.

reviews.review_description: Rendering is a bit slow, need to more tightly interest with Google Earth type data.  Gear VR controller is easy, but dedicated controller would feel better. Need more targets/goals in missions ala ultra wings experience. That all said, is a good idea to explore world this way and further development will mature the app.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your review.  The game is using WRLD3D data, not google maps, they similar but they are not the same... In places with massive data like in a city center, the map takes longer to load. you can reduce FOV in the setting. the controller has 2 modes, one is easy, and the second (Pilot) will give you more natural control, and it is not intended to make the game become harder. More mission will be available in the future, when more map areas will be avilable.

reviews.review_title: Everyone should support this and try it!
reviews.review_description: Great potential with dynamic mapping and flight experience - like a mini-FSX.  Impressive! Please continue to improve this... green tiles hurt immersion and wish they are replaced by flat 2d images or fog or faded out generic landscape. Collision detection of buildings would be nice.

developer_response.body: Thanks a lot for your review! It indeed encourge me to keep omproving :) I am working on an update with fog, collision with buildings will also be included as an option (like terrain collisions). Thanks for playin

reviews.review_description: Good game
Really nice experience. Working smoothly.

This is a very difficult game to give a star rating to. If I could I would give it a simultaneous 1 star and 5 stars, although I've opted for 5, in order to support the developer. I do really enjoy this game so bear with me why I explain why it's such a paradox.

First of all the bad: the physics/flight modelling. It's clear that the developer has spent very little time flying in real life or in a simulator, or at the very least has decided to omit this aspect entirely. The planes respond to the basic inputs of pitch, roll and yaw, but not in any physical way a plane on earth would. Every plane is modelled like a very stable missile. For instance if you roll the plane into a bank it will maintain that position indefinitely with no deviation from pointing directly forwards. Whether it's at 30°, 45°, 90°, completely inverted: no difference, you'll carry on in a perfectly straight line without turning, or losing altitude at all. In real life, planes turn by entering a little bank, which will then cause a turn, so the direction your nose is pointing will gently track across the horizon. This is basic flight dynamics. Instead in this game you have to use the rudder which will make your nose yaw, then when you release it the nose will remain perfectly pointing in that direction. In real life the nose will swing back to where you were initially pointing, after making large rudder inputs. In this game, increasing/decreasing throttle won't make any change in pitch or altitude. These aren't difficult/taxing things to implement: I'm not asking for full X-plane hi fidelity flight models, just basic stuff that differentiates a plane from a sprite moving across a screen. And let's not even get started on how both sets of ailerons are animated moving down when you pull the nose up. (Hint: the ailerons are for banking the wings, the little movey things that change the pitch (elevators) are at the back of the plane).

I'm not going to talk about the graphics as many other people have already done that, suffice to say you'll either love them or hate them. Personally I'm very fond of the cartoony yet relatively accurate representations of London and New York, despite the very small preload distances (hint: fly low). I get decent FPS, and there are a ton of objects being rendered.

Having said all that, this game is actually a huge lot of fun. There is sonething really immersive and enjoyable about flying above such a brightly colored 'cartoon Google maps', and there is something really fun and silly about the familiar yet surreal world. You get used to the insanity of the flight model very quickly, then you realise how cheaply you got it for and how much work must have gone into making such a Frankenstein. It's really worth the money, and as somebody pointed out, right bow it's the most ambitious project in the store. This guy is an early adopter, hopefully with time he can develop it into something terrific. He also is really responsive on the comments. I urge him to spend some time in X-plane or FSX though, just to get a basic grounding in how planes behave.

developer_response.body:
Hi! Thanks for your detailed review, I agree with your observation with the flight issue and will try soon a new method for the aircraft control, which supposes to give a more natural flight behavior. As Skytrek was initially developed I was simply trying to give the feeling of flying above real-world maps. But as I see there are more demands of the aircraft behavior, I will focus on that matter. there are more issues I am working on, such as better frame rate, better looking, and some more aircrafts skins. 
Thanks a lot for your support! this is really encouraging me to keep on working SkyTrek, I do believe myself, that this is just the start of an ongoing development, which will become better with more power on the VR devices, updates from the maps data provider, and more work from my side to set it all together. 
Thanks again, please visit my page:www.wingsgames.com for more :)

reviews.review_description: I hate it.  Don’t waste your money like I did.
I can't get GPS to work it keeps saying turn on location services. I have within the app permissions on the Oculus Go and on my phone but not working

reviews.review_title: Fantastic update but still few glitches
reviews.review_description: I have retracted my previous review with excellence you have updated and I am extremely happy that you did not only did the other locations open up but the graphics improved when I was in London flying over London I felt like I was actually looking at real London. Also Canada looks very real.Thank you for your prompt solution I highly enjoyed this game I suggest his game to anyone who wants to feel it if they are truly flying. I do wish that some of the controls on the panel worked and I also wish you put lights in the runways at times even while looking I have to circle around the airport in order for the graphics to show me where the airports are the runways should be lit up at night time thank you once again for the update and fixing the problems I wish this program and the developer much success.￼￼￼￼￼  I would like to add there are only six locations you can go to I hope you’re planning on adding more places around the world such as China and Australia. Also if there’s a way to light up the runways at night time brighter that would help with landing. I find that I have to recycle the airport in order to see the runways. Also￼ when using the Learjet the weight point goes off whack you might want to work on that. It doesn’t show you where the airport is you can see it from the sky but the way point goes off in fact it shows Africa instead of New York please look into that thank you. The waypoint for the LX B 600 JET shows Africa on the Altimeter and not the correct waypoint. Please fix that.

developer_response.body: Hello! thanks for your detailed review! I am going through it and will try to add as many features as possible for the next update. thanks again, and have a nice flight :)
